docs.title: Delete food entry
docs.text:
DELETE /api/day-entries/:id?deviceId=...

Deletes a food entry for the current device.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. User interacts with frontend
  2. Frontend sends requests to backend API
  3. Backend processes data
  4. Data is stored in database
  5. Response is sent back to frontend
  6. UI updates automatically

docs.title: Device ID
docs.text:
Each user is identified by a deviceId stored in the browser.

This ensures: - Data is separated per user - No login is required

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
The application runs in Docker containers:

  • frontend
  • backend
  • database
  • nginx

This ensures consistent environment.

docs.title: API returns 502
docs.text:
Problem: - Backend is not reachable

Solution: - Check if backend container is running - Check Nginx configuration - Check logs

docs.title: Data not updating
docs.text:
Problem: - UI does not refresh

Solution: - Reload page - Check frontend console

docs.title: Database connection error
docs.text:
Problem: - Backend cannot connect to database

Solution: - Check DATABASE_URL - Check if PostgreSQL is running

docs.title: Grafana shows DOWN
docs.text:
Problem: - Health endpoint is failing

Solution: - Check /api/health - Restart backend

docs.title: Add product
docs.text:
1. Open the "Add" page
  2. Enter product name
  3. Enter calories, protein, carbs, fat
  4. Click "Save Product"

docs.title: Add food entry
docs.text:
1. Open "Today"
  2. Choose a meal (breakfast, lunch, dinner)
  3. Click "+"
  4. Select a product
  5. Enter amount (grams)
  6. Click "Add"

docs.title: Delete food entry
docs.text:
1. Open "Today"
  2. Find the food entry
  3. Click "Delete"
  4. The entry is removed
  5. The summary updates automatically

docs.title: Privacy
docs.text: Food entries are stored in the database and linked to your browser using a deviceId.

docs.title: Consent
docs.text:
By using the application, the user agrees that:

  • Food data is stored in the database
  • Data is linked to the browser (deviceId)
